name: Prashant Rao
subtitle: B.Tech (2024) · IIT Indore
summary:
Civil engineering degree, software engineering career — it wasn't really a plan. I started competitive programming at IIT Indore, hit 5-star on CodeChef and Specialist on Codeforces, and by the time I graduated I'd spent more hours in a code editor than AutoCAD.

The past two years have been fintech and HR-tech. At Perusal I joined as one of the first engineers and built most of the product from scratch — candidate search on Elasticsearch, an end-to-end interview scheduling system, recruiter tooling, and a Lambda-based onboarding pipeline I eventually got down from 90s to 10s. At BlinkMoney I owned the wealth product end to end, wiring up lump-sum, SIP, and daily SIP flows through Fintech Primitives APIs and fixing a KYC pipeline that was quietly creating duplicate transactions on every retry.

I work across the stack but I'm strongest on the backend — NestJS, PostgreSQL, AWS. I like problems that are annoying to get right: payment idempotency, search relevance, things that break in ways that don't show up in unit tests.

highlights: Reworked the KYC sign-up flow by reusing links and tuning database queries, cutting API calls by ~70% and reducing duplicate transactions.
Improved the lien-marking service increasing loan-creation success by ~20%.
Built and shipped wealth-management features end to end — lump-sum, SIP, and daily SIP — by connecting Fintech Primitive APIs with NestJS and PostgreSQL.
Optimized the AWS Lambda + EventBridge client-onboarding pipeline, cutting run time from 90s to 10s (~88% faster).
Designed and built REST APIs and backend services using NestJS, MySQL, JWT authentication, and role-based access control, including an automated interview scheduling system.
Designed candidate search and filtering using Elasticsearch/OpenSearch, enabling full-text search, ranking, and faster resume discovery.
Automated job-description creation using AI, reducing manual writing effort by ~90%.
Led the frontend (React, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS) and shipped a reusable UI component library on NPM.
Added unit and integration tests (Jest, React Testing Library) across components and core flows to harden reliability.
Contributed to fine-tuning a globally leading LLM by applying domain expertise in Reinforcement Learning via Human Feedback (RLHF).
Enhanced model accuracy and real-world applicability, driving improvements in AI performance and user interaction quality.
Built and shipped UI/UX-driven full-stack web applications for clients at a design and development studio.

projects.name: LumenAI
projects.description: A privacy-first AI assistant that runs language models locally using Ollama on a Next.js, NestJS, and TypeScript stack.
highlights: Implemented semantic memory using PostgreSQL and pgvector for vector similarity search and retrieval of relevant historical conversations.
Built a retrieval and prompt orchestration pipeline that injects relevant memories into LLM context windows for personalized responses.

achievements.title: AIR 3 – WE CODE CHALLENGE
achievements.detail: Organised by Graphic Era Hill University · 2K+ participants
